title: herniaria vulgaris var. hirsuta
intro: Herniaria vulgaris var. hirsuta, also known as hairy sweet grass, is a variety of the common rue-leaved carnation, a small, annual plant found in Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of hairy sweet grass include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Hairy sweet grass can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and has also been used in traditional herbal remedies, such as teas and poultices.
